title: rubus lambertianus subsp. hakonensis
intro: Rubus lambertianus subsp. hakonensis, also known as Japanese bramble or Hakone black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus lambertianus plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and other polyphenols. The health benefits of Rubus lambertianus subsp. hakonensis include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in the production of jams, jellies, and other food products.
